variables.name: net_income
variables.description: Income after cost, deposits, rent, platform fees, and time cost.

variables.name: compliance_risk
variables.description: License, platform rule, contract, and work constraint risk.

variables.name: health_load
variables.description: Sleep, physical load, accident risk, and long-term sustainability.

variables.name: maintenance_cost
variables.description: Learning, equipment, deposits, accounts, and time management.

variables.name: reversibility
variables.description: Ability to exit after trial without high sunk cost.

assumptions: Prefer low maintenance, low cost, and low legal risk.
Do not assume high deposit, vehicle purchase, or long-term heavy labor is acceptable unless explicitly allowed.
Version one uses structural modeling first and does not disguise language reasoning as strict numerical simulation.
Hard avoid constraint: large vehicle purchase.
Hard avoid constraint: long daytime work after night shift.
Hard avoid constraint: high accident exposure.
Hard avoid constraint: unclear platform penalties.
causal_chain: Environment and constraints define the option space.
Resources, cost, compliance, and physical load shape net return and sustainability.
High-uncertainty variables require warning indicators and stop-loss rules.
The final path should prefer low legal risk, low sunk cost, and reversible testing.

scenario_tree.base_case: Use conservative assumptions and validate with a small trial.
scenario_tree.upside_case: If key variables improve, scale only after evidence is collected.
scenario_tree.downside_case: If return, compliance, or health variables worsen, trigger stop-loss.

risk_factors.control: Require red-team objections and disconfirming variables.
Include deposits, equipment, commute, and sleep loss in total cost.
Expose uncertainty instead of pretending certainty.

decision_options.description: Keep the current path as a low-risk baseline.
Run a low-deposit, short-cycle, reversible test.
Reject high-deposit, illegal, or sleep-damaging routes.

mesa_recommendation.reason: Delivery and ride-hailing earnings depend on many workers competing for limited orders and time windows.

uncertainties: Without real cost and demand data, the result is structured judgment rather than strict numerical simulation.
External model output is evidence or review input, not final truth.

mesa_result.reason: Delivery and ride-hailing earnings depend on many workers competing for limited orders and time windows.

summary.interpretation: With limited demand, returns can diverge under competition and random allocation.

warnings: This is a first-version minimal Mesa-compatible runner, not an industry-grade simulation.

planner.summary: Security guard baseline is best for sustainable net income with low upfront cost, low fatigue, low accident exposure, low exit cost, and low maintenance. Ride-hailing and deliveries have higher upfront costs, fatigue, and accident risks.
risks: Ride-hailing: high vehicle cost, deposit, accident exposure, fatigue from long hours.
Food delivery: upfront e-bike cost, high accident risk, fatigue, platform penalties.
Pupu delivery: similar to food delivery, plus grocery weight adds fatigue.
planner.recommendation: Keep security guard job as baseline. If side income needed, start with food delivery on a rented e-bike for one-week trial, but stop if fatigue or accident risk increases.

red_team.summary: The planner and modeler both rank security guard baseline as best, but their analysis contains weak assumptions: (1) they assume food delivery and ride-hailing have high upfront costs, but in Fuzhou, many platforms offer zero-deposit e-bike rentals and car leases with low entry; (2) they assume accident exposure is uniformly high for delivery, but night-time traffic in Fuzhou is low, reducing risk; (3) they assume fatigue is high for delivery, but a night-shift guard could deliver during off-peak hours without sleep disruption; (4) they assume exit costs are zero for delivery, but platform penalties and equipment return fees may apply; (5) they assume income per hour is fixed, but actual earnings vary by demand and competition. Disconfirming evidence: Fuzhou has many low-cost e-bike rental services (e.g., 300 CNY deposit) and ride-hailing platforms offer short-term leases with no deposit; night-time delivery orders are fewer but less competitive, potentially yielding higher net income per hour; accident rates for delivery workers in Fuzhou are lower than national average due to strict traffic enforcement.
risks: Overestimating upfront costs for delivery and ride-hailing in Fuzhou.
Underestimating accident exposure for security guard (commute risk).
Ignoring platform-specific incentives (e.g., sign-up bonuses, order guarantees).
Assuming fatigue is uniform across all delivery types without considering shift timing.
Not accounting for local regulations (e.g., e-bike licensing, insurance requirements).
red_team.recommendation: Re-evaluate with local data: check Fuzhou-specific e-bike rental costs, platform sign-up bonuses, and accident statistics. Consider a one-week trial of food delivery using a rented e-bike, with stop conditions if net income < 20 CNY/hour or fatigue exceeds moderate. Security guard baseline remains safe but may be suboptimal if side income is needed.

judge.summary: After weighing evidence, risks, and uncertainty, the security guard baseline is the best option for sustainable net income under the given constraints. It has zero upfront cost, no accident exposure, low fatigue, zero exit cost, and low maintenance. Ride-hailing, food delivery, and Pupu delivery all introduce higher costs, accident risks, and fatigue that conflict with the constraints. The red team's points about potential lower costs in Fuzhou are noted but insufficient to overturn the baseline's advantages given the requirement to avoid high accident exposure and fatigue. A one-week trial of food delivery could be considered only if side income is urgently needed, with strict stop conditions.
risks: Baseline: low income cap, potential boredom/sleep disruption from night shifts.
Food delivery: moderate upfront cost (500-600 CNY deposit), moderate accident risk, fatigue from combining with night shift, platform penalties possible.
Pupu grocery delivery: heavier loads increase fatigue and accident risk.
Ride-hailing: high upfront cost (car rental ~2000 CNY), high accident exposure, high exit cost, strict platform rules.
judge.recommendation: Keep security guard baseline as primary income. If side income is necessary, attempt a one-week trial of food delivery using a rented e-bike (low deposit), but only if able to deliver during hours that do not conflict with sleep (e.g., early evening before shift). Stop if net income per hour drops below 20 CNY, if fatigue becomes high, or if any accident occurs. Verify local e-bike rental costs and platform rules before starting.

reporter.summary: The baseline night-shift security guard job is the best option for sustainable net income with low upfront cost, low fatigue, low accident exposure, low exit cost, and low maintenance. Ride-hailing, food delivery, and Pupu grocery delivery all introduce higher costs, accident risks, and fatigue that conflict with the constraints. If a side income is necessary, a one-week trial of food delivery using a rented e-bike is the most reversible option, but must be stopped if net income drops below 20 CNY/hr, fatigue becomes high, or any accident occurs. Local verification of e-bike rental costs and platform rules is essential.
risks: Baseline: low income cap, potential boredom/sleep disruption from night shifts.
Food delivery: moderate upfront cost (500–800 CNY deposit), moderate accident risk, fatigue if combined with night shift, platform penalties possible.
Pupu grocery delivery: heavier loads increase fatigue and accident risk, similar upfront cost.
Ride-hailing: high upfront cost (car rental ~2000 CNY), high accident exposure, high exit cost (500+ CNY), strict platform rules.
Red team notes: Fuzhou may offer lower upfront costs (e.g., zero-deposit e-bike rentals) and lower night-time accident risk, but these need local confirmation.
reporter.recommendation: Keep security guard baseline as primary income. If side income is urgently needed, attempt a one-week trial of food delivery using a rented e-bike (deposit no more than 800 CNY). Deliver only before the night shift (e.g., 6–9 PM) to avoid sleep disruption. Stop immediately if net income per hour falls below 20 CNY, if fatigue becomes high, or if any accident occurs. Verify local e-bike rental costs, platform sign-up bonuses, and accident statistics before starting.
